text: Presenting a case of laparoscopic myomectomy in multiple myoma. A novel technique of suturing with a single barb suture is shown. I am Professor Alka Kiplani. I bring you greetings from Paras Hospital's Gurgaon.

This was a patient who complained of secondary infertility with AUVL and on examination uterus was found to be enlarged to fourteen to sixteen weeks pregnancy size Imaging showed multiple myomas and she was planned for laparoscopic myomectomy with hysteroscopy and tubal patency testing.
The laparoscopic view reveals multiple myomas in the uterus, with no visible adhesions. Chromotubation using methylene blue dye confirms the patency of both fallopian tubes.

The surgeon begins the myomectomy on a large sub-serosal fibroid, making an incision using either harmonic scalpel or scissors. Pitricin injection is administered to minimize bleeding, and scissors are used to achieve a precise dissection within the capsule of the myoma.

During the laparoscopic myomectomy, the surgeon carefully palpates and removes multiple myomas using a claw instrument for stabilization. The myomas are deposited in the pouch of Douglas or the paracolic gutter to prevent loss in the peritoneal cavity, ensuring all are accounted for and retrieved.

The surgeon uses a single barb suture, number zero V-Loc, to suture all the myomas subserosally from anterior to posterior. The suture technique is completed with one continuous suture, and the final view shows the sutured myomas.
